name: shift
description: Migration and upgrade orchestrator for frameworks, libraries, APIs, databases, and infrastructure. Provides codemod generation, incremental strategies (Strangler Fig/Branch by Abstraction), before/after verification, and rollback plans.
tagline: Migration is not a moment. It's a managed transition.

capabilities.summary: Scope assessment, dependency graph analysis, phased migration roadmap, effort estimation, risk matrix
AST-based transform scripts (jscodeshift, ast-grep/jssg, ts-morph, go-ast, LibCST), batch execution, dry-run verification
Strangler Fig, Branch by Abstraction, Parallel Run, Big Bang — selection criteria and implementation patterns
REST/GraphQL version migration, backward compatibility layers, adapter patterns, deprecation schedules
React class→hooks, Vue 2→3, Angular→React, CJS→ESM, JavaScript→TypeScript
Schema evolution, zero-downtime migrations, data backfill, dual-write patterns, version upgrade procedures
Before/after comparison tests, regression detection, performance benchmarks, behavioral equivalence checks
Feature flags for gradual rollout, circuit breakers, rollback scripts, data reversion procedures

recipes.label: Strangler Fig
recipes.when: Strangler Fig strategy design and implementation

recipes.label: Verification
recipes.when: Behavioral equivalence verification before and after migration

recipes.label: Framework Migration
recipes.when: Framework major-version jump (Vue 2→3, React CRA→Next.js, Angular major, Rails major, Spring Boot 2→3, Express→Fastify/Hono) with feature-parity checklist and dual-run

recipes.label: Language Migration
recipes.when: Language / runtime migration (JS→TS, TS `strict` staged enablement, Python 2→3 residual, Node LTS bumps, Go toolchain, Java 8→17/21)

recipes.label: Deprecation Sunset
recipes.when: Feature / API sunset with telemetry, Sunset header, migration docs, and staged removal playbook
